title: Confusing error message when using default_nettype none and a local is defined in unnamed block in verilog mode
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.33+79 (git sha1 b52f6cb19, gcc 13.2.1 -march=x86-64 -mtune=generic -O2 -fno-plt -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

1. Save this code to `bug.v`:

```
module bug();
  initial begin
    integer i;
    i = 0;
  end
endmodule
```

2. Run `yosys -p "read_verilog bug.v"`
3. Observe bad diagnostic message: "ERROR: Identifier  \`\i' is implicitly declared and \`default_nettype is set to none".

If default_nettype none is removed, then we get the expected error message: "ERROR: Local declaration in unnamed block is only supported in SystemVerilog mode!", added in https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/commit/81a9ee2360740dd05e6fc8215abd0e30837f2cda.

Local declarations in unnamed blocks is not allowed in old school verilog. I guess that causes the later usage to be on an undeclared variable, and that message has higher priority than the more useful message about the illegal local declaration, causing the bug.

### Expected Behavior

"ERROR: Local declaration in unnamed block is only supported in SystemVerilog mode!" is printed

### Actual Behavior

"ERROR: Identifier \`\i' is implicitly declared and \`default_nettype is set to none" is printed
